event: being followed up by a gastroenterologist
minor penetrating injury to his left lower leg from a cactus plant
Presentation to ED via ambulance for worsening left leg swelling from a recent cactus plant prick
Admitted to the intensive care unit (ICU)
intraoperative extensive left foot and below knee tissue necrosis
large areas of unviable skin and muscle
preparation for continuous renal replacement therapy (CRRT)
Initial blood cultures isolated Group B Streptococcus pneumoniae (GBSPn)
Intravenous Immunoglobin G (IVIgG) therapy 100 g initiated
taken back to theatre to confirm source control
viability of the left stump noted
new onset Rapid Atrial Fibrillation (RAF) with HR 110 bpm
remained oliguric 7-25 ml/hour despite ongoing hemofiltration
Echocardiography: Moderate segmental left ventricular dysfunction
Amiodarone loading dose 300 mg over 1 hour
Amiodarone maintenance dose 900 mg over 24 hours
CRRT (Day 2) – AN69 anti-inflammatory heparin laden adsorbing filter applied (Oxiris Filter)
Patient developed features consistent with Sepsis Induced Coagulopathy (ISTH -SIC criteria)
Wound culture for MCS had medium growth of GBSPn
Tissue culture (intraoperatively) had medium growth of GBSPn
Group B Streptococcus pneumoniae from tissue showed intermediate minimum inhibitory concentration to penicillin
Furosemide IV 250 mg stat to assess renal response
New onset purpura over the right forearm
multiple weeping skin tears on right upper thigh
Hyper-Ammonium Therapy initiated - Rifaximin NGT 550 mg BD
Hyper-Ammonium Therapy initiated - Lactulose NGT 20 ml TDS
remained deeply sedated despite being off sedation
New onset lateralizing signs to the left upon painful stimuli compared to the right side
CT Brain: No acute intracranial pathology
Significant deterioration despite ongoing medical therapy
Family was involved in this decision
consent for critical care supportive therapy was withdrawn
The patient was not deemed to be a coroner's case
challenged with combination of Furosemide IV 250 mg and Acetazolamide IV 500 mg
Lincomycin IV 600 mg TDS – recommenced as antitoxin
Group B Streptococcus pneumonia Necrotising Fasciitis
